symptoms.symptom_name: Dactylitis plus asymmetric warm knee effusion with nail pitting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dactylitis with asymmetric large-joint effusion and nail pitting is highly characteristic of PsA.
Simultaneous two-site asymmetric involvement is atypical for monoarticular joint sepsis.
Whole-digit dactylitis with nail changes is against focal podagra.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warm fluctuant knee effusion (hot joint) with low-grade fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A warm effused joint with fever is a hot joint that must be aspirated to exclude infection, even when PsA is likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single fiery/tense/unbearably hot knee; no high fever/rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a fiery tense joint and high fever/rigors lowers, but does not eliminate, septic probability — aspiration is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: No preceding GI/GU infection; established psoriasis/nail pitting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No preceding infection and established psoriasis point to PsA rather than reactive arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No skin portal of entry; not immunosuppressed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No obvious portal/immunosuppression modestly lowers septic risk, but aspiration remains the arbiter.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dactylitis MCP-DIP, violaceous digit; fluctuant warm knee effusion; stable ligaments; no portal (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dactylitis with an effused knee and nail changes fits oligoarticular PsA.
A warm knee effusion requires aspiration/synovial analysis to exclude infection.
Psoriasis/nail disease is the substrate for psoriatic arthritis.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Oligoarticular psoriatic arthritis (septic joint to exclude)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Dactylitis and an asymmetric warm knee effusion with nail pitting strongly suggest oligoarticular PsA, but a warm effused joint is a hot joint that must be aspirated to exclude septic arthritis — which can't be done remotely — so urgent same-day in-person assessment (± aspiration) with septic-joint safety-netting is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent same-day in-person joint assessment and knee aspiration (synovial microscopy/culture/crystals)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude septic arthritis and crystals in the warm effused knee; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBC, CRP, ESR, urate) ± blood cultures if febrile

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: For DMARD/biologic planning once infection is excluded.
